subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the basic concepts of production and its requirements
Learn about the farming and non-farming activities in a village
Understand the impact of the Green Revolution and modern farming methods
Analyze the distribution of land and labor in rural areas
Differentiate between fixed capital and working capital
Identify the role of human capital in production

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Palampur & Factors of Production
topics_covered: Four factors of production: Land, Labour, Physical Capital, and Human Capital
teaching_strategy: Narrative storytelling to describe the village
student_activities: Identify production factors in a local shop or farm
Classify items as Fixed or Working Capital
assessment: Oral quiz on the four requirements of production

lesson_plan.title: Farming in Palampur: Land and Irrigation
topics_covered: Ways to increase production: Multiple cropping
teaching_strategy: Comparative discussion on traditional vs. modern irrigation
Using diagrams to explain seasonal crop cycles
student_activities: Draw a seasonal crop calendar (Kharif and Rabi)
Discuss the impact of electricity on village life
assessment: Short written explanation of multiple cropping

lesson_plan.title: Modern Farming & the Green Revolution
teaching_strategy: Presentation of data comparing traditional and HYV yields
Video clip or photos of the Green Revolution in Punjab/Haryana
student_activities: List modern inputs required for farming (fertilizers, pesticides, etc.)
Calculate yield differences from textbook examples

lesson_plan.title: Sustainability and Land Distribution
topics_covered: Unequal distribution of land among families
teaching_strategy: Case study discussion on soil health
Using a pie chart to show land distribution
student_activities: Analyze a pie chart showing land ownership in Palampur
Debate: Can modern farming be sustainable?
assessment: Paragraph writing on soil fertility depletion

lesson_plan.title: Labour and Capital in Farming
topics_covered: Who provides the labor? (Small vs. Medium/Large farmers)
Capital requirements and sources of credit
The debt trap for small farmers
teaching_strategy: Role-play between a small farmer and a moneylender
Flowchart of capital flow for large farmers
student_activities: List sources of loans for different types of farmers
assessment: Comparison table of small vs. large farmers' capital

lesson_plan.title: Non-Farm Activities in Palampur
teaching_strategy: Discussion on diversification of the rural economy
student_activities: Identify non-farm activities in their own locality
Write a short note on Kareem’s computer center example
assessment: Short answer test on non-farm activities

lesson_plan.title: Summary, Revision, and Evaluation
topics_covered: Review of production factors and farming methods
Summary of social and economic life in Palampur
student_activities: Complete a summary mind map in notebooks

teaching_aids: Images of HYV seeds and modern farm machinery
teacher_tip: Connect the fictional village of Palampur to the students' nearest local village or market to make the economic concepts of 'capital' and 'labor' more relatable.
